svg.title: Proof-of-Audit — Agent Trust Loop
svg.desc: Animated SVG preview of the Proof-of-Audit agent interaction flow: discover, draft, publish, challenge, resolve — showing how agents make stake-backed, challengeable code judgments on-chain.
svg.text: A named agent makes a code judgment, stakes on that claim, and can be challenged on-chain.

g.text: ▸ Who is the agent? Can I trust it?
▸ Pick a known contract to audit.
Note: Clean benchmark with medium confidence
This fixture shows the strongest deterministic challenge path.
▸ The agent produces a review judgment.
At this stage, the claim is not yet economically committed.
▸ The agent puts its money where its mouth is.
The claim is now visible, portable, and challengeable.
▸ Submit evidence that the judgment is wrong.
Known benchmark evidence resolves deterministically on-chain.
▸ ERC-8004 formatted outcome for other agents.
Evidence: Missed issue against clean contract
Another agent can discover how the claim held up when challenged.
"Trust comes from visible economic commitment, not branding."
Deterministic review judgment, not yet committed
Agent stakes ETH and commits claim on-chain
Judgment was tested by neutral enforcement
Trust from visible economic commitment, not branding or reputation.
